formulas.name: The Law of the Sea
formulas.formula: No specific formula, but it emphasizes the importance of freedom of the seas
formulas.variables: Freedom of the seas, maritime trade, and navigation
formulas.notes: A principle that governed international relations and maritime trade

formulas.name: The 17th and 18th-Century Maritime Laws
formulas.formula: No specific formula, but they emphasized the importance of navigation and trade laws
formulas.variables: Navigation laws, trade laws, and colonial policies
formulas.notes: Laws that governed maritime trade, navigation, and colonial policies during the 17th and 18th centuries

formulas.name: The Treaty of Tordesillas (1494)
formulas.formula: No specific formula, but it divided the world into Spanish and Portuguese spheres of influence
formulas.notes: A treaty that divided the world into Spanish and Portuguese spheres of influence, marking the beginning of European exploration and colonization

formulas.name: The 19th-Century European Imperialism
formulas.formula: No specific formula, but it emphasized the importance of colonial expansion and the scramble for Africa
formulas.variables: Colonial expansion, imperialism, and the scramble for Africa
formulas.notes: A period of intense colonial expansion and the scramble for Africa, marked by the partitioning of the continent among European powers

formulas.name: The Berlin Conference (1884-1885)
formulas.formula: No specific formula, but it led to the partitioning of Africa
formulas.variables: Africa, European powers, and colonial expansion
formulas.notes: A conference that led to the partitioning of Africa, marking the beginning of the scramble for Africa and the end of the period of free trade
